perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERM: November 01, 2014 to October 31, 2017, unless terminated earlier as per section 16.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding an exception to the contract's term length. While section 6 states the agreement ends on October 31, 2017, the added phrase references a hypothetical 'section 16' (which does not actually exist in the original document) that allows for earlier termination. This contradicts the fixed term specified in section 6, creating uncertainty about the contract's actual duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. TERM: November 01, 2014 to October 31, 2017, unless terminated earlier as per section 16.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 adds a condition for early termination ('unless terminated earlier as per section 16') that introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original fixed term ending date. Since Section 16 does not exist in the original document, it creates an ambiguity about the contract's actual duration, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. OTHER PROVISIONS: It is agreed that no personal liability whatsoever shall attach to, be imposed on or otherwise be incurred by any Partner, agent, management official or employee of the Transporter or any director, officer or employee of any of the foregoing, for any obligation of the Transporter arising under this Agreement or for any claim based on such obligation and that the sole recourse of Shipper under this Agreement is limited to assets of the Transporter.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. OTHER PROVISIONS: It is agreed that no personal liability whatsoever shall attach to, be imposed on or otherwise be incurred by any Partner, agent, management official or employee of the Transporter or any director, officer or employee of any of the foregoing, for any obligation of the Transporter arising under this Agreement or for any claim based on such obligation and that the sole recourse of Shipper under this Agreement is limited to assets of the Transporter, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct as determined by a court of law.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause creates a contradiction. Initially, the contract states the Shipper's recourse is *solely* limited to the assets of the Transporter and that no personal liability can be imposed on any personnel associated with the Transporter. However, the added exception stating that personal liability *can* arise in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct directly contradicts the initial statement. This creates a contradiction over the extent of personal liability and enforcement, making it unclear under which conditions personal liability can be imposed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. OTHER PROVISIONS: It is agreed that no personal liability whatsoever shall attach to, be imposed on or otherwise be incurred by any Partner, agent, management official or employee of the Transporter or any director, officer or employee of any of the foregoing, for any obligation of the Transporter arising under this Agreement or for any claim based on such obligation and that the sole recourse of Shipper under this Agreement is limited to assets of the Transporter, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct as determined by a court of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the initial clause that stated no personal liability could be imposed on personnel associated with the Transporter and that the Shipper's recourse was solely limited to the assets of the Transporter. By adding "except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct as determined by a court of law," the changed text now allows for personal liability under specific circumstances, directly contradicting the original assertion of no personal liability whatsoever. As the contradicted text and changed text are one and the same, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time. However, in the event of a direct conflict between the terms of this agreement and the FERC Gas Tariff, the terms of a separate side letter agreement, dated December 15, 2015, shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction because it creates uncertainty regarding which document governs the agreement. Section 12 initially states that the FERC Gas Tariff is incorporated and binding. However, the added text introduces a previously unmentioned 'side letter agreement' that takes precedence over the FERC Gas Tariff in case of conflict. This directly contradicts the primary incorporation clause, creating ambiguity regarding which terms prevail and introducing a document not previously referenced, leading to potential disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time. However, in the event of a direct conflict between the terms of this agreement and the FERC Gas Tariff, the terms of a separate side letter agreement, dated December 15, 2015, shall govern.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'side letter agreement' that supersedes the FERC Gas Tariff in case of conflict. This directly contradicts the original incorporation clause, which states the FERC Gas Tariff governs the agreement. This creates a clear inconsistency and contradiction, as it introduces ambiguity about which terms prevail and a document not previously referenced, directly impacting contractual obligations, hence it is a strong contradiction. The contradictory text is also in the same location.
